DAVID P. 
DANIEL V. HYDE 
LAW OFFICES 
~NAP~ STEVENS, GROSSMAN & MARSH 
1800 UNITED CALIFORNIA BANK BUILDING 
707 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD 
LOS ANGELES 90017 
TELEPHONE ~213J 627-8471 
December 13, 1977 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 Kay Street 
Sacramento, California 95814 
RE
T:' .. 
The Southern California Coastal Water Research 
Project Authority, a Joint Powers Agency 
Gentlemen: 
WYMAN C. KNAPP 
OF COUNSEL 
.JOSEPH C. GILL 
1912- 1968 
HUGH GORDON 
i884~1974 
On two previous occasions we have written to you to inquire whether 
or not the Commission had established a deadline for the adoption 
of a Conflict of Interest Code that would be applicable to our above 
referenced client. 
In your bulletin dated November 18, 1977, you commented on Opinion 
76078. 
You were then asked whether the Encina Joint Sewerage System 
and the Joint Advisory Committee of the Encina Joint Sewerage System 
are governmental agencies which are required to adopt Conflict of 
Interest Codes. 
You apparently dismissed the Opinion on the basis of 
a previously approved staff advice that neither entity is a govern-
mental agency required to adopt such a Code. 
It would be appreciated if you would advise me of the basis for the 
Opinion so that we might determine whether or not it is also appli-
cable to the Southern California Coastal Water Research Project 
Authority. 
Very truly yours, 
B. RICHARD MARSH 
BRM/la 
cc· 
Southern California Coastal vJater Research 
Project Authori

sections.question_synthetic: The attorney is asking whether the Southern California Coastal Water Research Project Authority, a Joint Powers Agency, is required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under FPPC regulations, and seeking clarification on how a previous opinion regarding the Encina Joint Sewerage System applies to their client.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter, not an FPPC response. No verbatim question or conclusion sections are present. The document references previous correspondence and a prior FPPC opinion (76078) regarding a related entity. Document quality is poor with some text degradation, but core content is identifiable.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney representing the Southern California Coastal Water Research Project Authority seeking FPPC guidance on whether their client is a governmental agency required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code, and requesting clarification on the basis for a prior dismissal of a similar opinion.
